generated_comment: Neha, I made the profile because tum 28 ki ho chuki ho, this is not the age to be casual about these things. I have put basic filters only—decent family, settled boy, same community—because baad mein achhe matches itne easily nahi milte for girls. You can be upset now, but Mummy-Papa are right, shaadi ke matter mein aise time waste nahi karte.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it pressures a woman into marriage based on age and frames her value as declining over time, especially 'for girls.' It encodes casual patriarchal control over her choices and treats family/community filtering as normal authority over her marriage.
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 38. Married. A man in Delhi. You are on a matrimonial website helping your younger sister Neha who is 28 create a profile because your parents have been asking you to handle it since Neha is not taking it seriously herself. You are on a phone call with Neha telling her you have created the profile and what you have written and what filters you have set for prospective matches. Neha is not happy that you did this without asking her. You are telling her why you did it anyway and why at 28 she does not have the luxury of not taking this seriously
